Avenger of Zendikar.overall_comment: Avenger of Zendikar is a major lands payoff here, turning the deck’s heavy land count and continued land drops into a wide board that can quickly become a game-ending attack, though it asks for substantial mana and follow-up lands to reach full pressure.

roles.description: Avenger of Zendikar converts this deck’s land-heavy board and ongoing landfall into a large token army that scales with future land drops and supports the deck’s Craterhoof-style finishing plan.

Beast Within.overall_comment: Beast Within gives this deck highly flexible instant-speed spot removal for any problematic permanent, with strong timing and broad coverage tempered by leaving behind a 3/3 body.

roles.description: Beast Within is broad instant-speed removal that cleanly answers any permanent type this deck may face, though the replacement 3/3 token softens the trade compared with cleaner one-for-one answers.

Boseiju, Who Endures.overall_comment: Boseiju, Who Endures is a highly efficient untapped green land for this deck that also doubles as flexible instant-speed removal for key noncreature permanents and opposing utility lands, with its answer limited to artifacts, enchantments, and nonbasic lands.

roles.description: An untapped green source is excellent for this green-heavy deck, and its built-in channel mode gives a land slot meaningful interaction against problematic artifacts, enchantments, and nonbasic lands at low deck-building cost.

Its channel ability provides instant-speed one-for-one removal from a land slot with broad coverage against key noncreature permanents, though the replacement land for the opponent softens the exchange.

Brainstorm.overall_comment: Brainstorm gives this deck cheap instant-speed card smoothing that can line up lands, interaction, or payoffs efficiently, though its impact stays modest when repeated top-deck clearing is limited.

roles.description: Brainstorm is a very efficient instant-speed filter that sees three cards and lets this deck reorder two back, helping sculpt draws for lands, ramp, protection, or finishers with strong timing but modest long-game setup depth.

Command Tower.overall_comment: Command Tower is a premier untapped fixer for this two-color Tatyova deck, supporting early green ramp and later blue-green sequencing with excellent consistency, though its contribution stays focused on mana production.

roles.description: Command Tower fills a land slot with near-perfect untapped color fixing for this two-color deck, making early green development and later blue requirements easy while contributing little beyond mana.

Counterspell.overall_comment: Counterspell gives this deck efficient instant-speed stack interaction that can stop nearly any opposing spell at a key moment, with the main limitation of requiring steady blue mana to hold up.

roles.description: Counterspell is a highly efficient instant-speed one-for-one that cleanly answers almost any spell before it resolves, with broad coverage and strong flexibility tempered by its double-blue holding pattern.

Craterhoof Behemoth.overall_comment: Craterhoof Behemoth is a premier finisher here, converting the deck's developed creature board into an immediate trample alpha strike, though its impact depends on already having enough bodies in play.

roles.description: Craterhoof Behemoth is an elite closer for this deck, turning a wide board from cards like Avenger of Zendikar into a hasty trample swing that often ends the game immediately once enough creatures are assembled.

Cultivate.overall_comment: Cultivate is a solid permanent-mana ramp spell here, advancing toward Tatyova and later land-heavy turns with stable color fixing, though the tapped battlefield land makes its acceleration pace fairly typical.

roles.description: Cultivate provides durable acceleration by putting a basic onto the battlefield and another into hand, helping this deck hit Tatyova and keep making land drops, with typical speed and a tapped-land tempo cost.

Cyclonic Rift.overall_comment: Cyclonic Rift serves this deck as highly flexible instant-speed interaction early and a powerful one-sided reset late, though its answer is temporary and its overloaded mode comes online at a premium cost.

roles.description: Overloaded, Cyclonic Rift can reset all opposing nonland permanents while leaving this lands-heavy board intact, creating a strongly asymmetric swing that this deck can exploit to push ahead or set up a finishing attack.
At two mana, Cyclonic Rift cleanly answers almost any opposing nonland permanent at instant speed, with excellent flexibility for protecting tempo even though the answer sends threats back to hand.

Eternal Witness.overall_comment: Eternal Witness supplies solid card advantage by returning the best card in your graveyard while leaving behind a body, with its ceiling tied to having a worthwhile graveyard target and enough mana to recast it.

roles.description: Eternal Witness nets a card on entry by returning any card from your graveyard to hand while keeping a creature on board, giving this deck dependable access to its best spent ramp, interaction, or payoff pieces.

Evolving Wilds.overall_comment: Evolving Wilds fills a land slot with decent color access and meaningful utility for this Tatyova deck by converting into a basic land and generating an extra land-entry sequence over time, though the tapped sequencing and self-sacrifice slow its tempo.

roles.description: Evolving Wilds fixes either deck color and has useful utility in a lands-matter shell through its self-sacrifice into a basic, though entering as a land that must be cashed in for tapped mana makes it slower than stronger fixing lands.

Forest.overall_comment: Forest is a highly efficient, resilient green source that supports this deck’s land-heavy development and Tatyova mana needs, with little utility beyond dependable untapped mana.

roles.description: Forest is an untapped, highly resilient green source that fits this green-heavy Tatyova deck smoothly, with excellent baseline slot efficiency and only minimal utility beyond producing mana.

Harmonize.overall_comment: Harmonize gives this lands deck a dependable midgame refill of three real cards, with strong floor and card quality but no continuing engine beyond the one shot.

roles.description: Harmonize is a clean one-shot refill that turns one card into three fresh cards, giving this deck reliable midgame fuel with strong floor, though it produces its value only once at sorcery speed.

Heroic Intervention.overall_comment: Heroic Intervention protects this deck’s landfall engines, mana base, and token finishers at instant speed, with excellent timing and broad shielding that depends on already having key permanents in play.

roles.description: Heroic Intervention is a strong protection piece for this deck’s permanent-heavy engine, covering Tatyova, extra lands, and wide payoff boards at instant speed, though it matters most once you have established permanents worth saving.

Midnight Tilling.overall_comment: Midnight Tilling serves this deck as cheap instant-speed card selection that digs four cards deep and can recover a milled permanent, with its value capped by finding only one card and only from among those four.

roles.description: At instant speed it digs four cards deep and can convert the best permanent among them into hand, giving this lands-heavy deck flexible access to lands or key engine pieces for only two mana.

Oracle of Mul Daya.overall_comment: Oracle of Mul Daya is a strong lands engine piece here, accelerating mana through extra land drops while turning the top of the library into steady land access; its main limitation is that the acceleration depends on having lands available and keeping a fragile four-mana creature in play.

roles.description: Oracle of Mul Daya gives this deck repeatable acceleration through an extra land play each turn, lining up well with a land-heavy Tatyova shell, though it needs lands in hand or on top and survives as a vulnerable creature.

Oracle of Mul Daya directly advances this deck’s lands-matter engine by increasing landfall frequency and converting the top of the library into more land plays, a role that reaches far beyond generic green ramp in this shell.

Revealing the top card and letting you play lands from there improves draw quality by clearing lands off the top, giving this deck ongoing topdeck smoothing that helps reach engine pieces and action more consistently.

Ponder.overall_comment: Ponder gives this Tatyova deck cheap early smoothing to line up lands, ramp, engine pieces, or interaction, with strong efficiency but only a modest digging range from a single look at three cards.

roles.description: Ponder is an efficient one-mana cantrip that filters the next draws, helping this deck find lands, ramp, Tatyova, or protection early while only seeing a small slice of the library at once.

Rampant Growth.overall_comment: Rampant Growth is efficient permanent ramp that fixes both colors, advances this lands-focused curve toward Tatyova, and trades some tempo for a tapped land.

roles.description: Rampant Growth gives this deck an early extra land on the battlefield with solid color fixing and strong alignment with casting Tatyova on time, though the tapped land trims some immediate tempo.

Ramunap Excavator.overall_comment: Ramunap Excavator is a plan-specific enabler that turns lands in the graveyard into continued land drops for Tatyova and rebuilds the deck's engine well, though it needs lands to reach the graveyard before its value becomes substantial.

roles.description: Ramunap Excavator directly fuels this lands-matter engine by replaying lands from the graveyard, extending landfall with Tatyova and helping the deck recover key mana development after interaction, though its impact depends on graveyarded lands already being available.

Regrowth.overall_comment: Regrowth gives this deck efficient, broad graveyard-to-hand selection that reliably rebuy key lands, ramp, protection, engines, answers, or finishers at any stage, with power capped by costing a card for a single return.

roles.description: Regrowth is highly flexible one-shot selection here, turning any card in your graveyard into the best missing piece for the moment, from a land or ramp spell to protection, Tatyova support, or a finisher.

Reliquary Tower.overall_comment: Reliquary Tower serves this deck as a low-tempo-cost land whose main upside is preserving the large hand sizes Tatyova can generate, though its colorless mana makes it a narrower land slot in a blue-green list.

roles.description: It enters untapped and its no-maximum-hand-size text can preserve a Tatyova-fueled grip, but producing only colorless mana makes it a narrower fit than colored sources in this two-color deck.

Rhystic Study.overall_comment: Rhystic Study is a strong repeatable card-advantage engine for this Tatyova deck, keeping resources flowing across opponents’ turns with a powerful ceiling that still depends on opponents’ willingness or ability to pay the tax.

roles.description: Rhystic Study is an above-average long-game draw engine here, generating repeated extra cards off opponents’ spellcasting with excellent card quality, though its output varies with table behavior and removal.

Sakura-Tribe Elder.overall_comment: Sakura-Tribe Elder is early permanent ramp that fixes colors through a basic land entering the battlefield, fitting the deck’s curve well while costing a card and a creature body for a single tapped-land boost.

roles.description: Sakura-Tribe Elder ramps early by turning into a basic land on the battlefield, giving permanent acceleration and color fixing that helps reach Tatyova on time, though the gain is only one tapped land for a card.

Simic Signet.overall_comment: Simic Signet is early, repeatable two-color ramp that helps this lands deck cast Tatyova on time, though its activation cost makes the acceleration a touch slower than the best two-mana options.

roles.description: Simic Signet is solid early ramp that fixes both deck colors and helps bridge into Tatyova, though needing an extra mana to activate makes its acceleration slightly slower than the strongest two-mana rocks.

Sol Ring.overall_comment: Sol Ring is elite early repeatable acceleration that helps this lands deck jump quickly into Tatyova and its expensive finishers, though its colorless output keeps the fixing piece of that ramp only average.

roles.description: Sol Ring provides extremely fast, permanent acceleration that jumps this deck toward Tatyova and top-end landfall turns very efficiently, with colorless mana fitting acceptably but not perfectly in a blue-green shell.

Solemn Simulacrum.overall_comment: Solemn Simulacrum serves this deck mainly as steady permanent ramp that advances land count for Tatyova while arriving late and at a modest acceleration rate.

roles.description: It provides permanent ramp by putting a basic land onto the battlefield, which supports Tatyova and future land-based turns well, though four mana makes it slower and less efficient than stronger ramp options.

Sylvan Library.overall_comment: Sylvan Library is a highly efficient card-selection engine for this Tatyova lands deck, smoothing draws every turn to find lands, ramp, protection, and finishers while only becoming true extra cards when life can be spent.

roles.description: Sylvan Library gives excellent repeated top-of-draw-step selection, letting this deck continually curate draws toward lands, engine pieces, interaction, and closing threats for only two mana.

Tatyova, Benthic Druid.overall_comment: Tatyova is a premier landfall card-advantage engine for this lands deck, turning the deck’s frequent land entries into a steady flow of real cards with a strong floor once lands keep coming.

roles.description: Tatyova converts this deck’s heavy land count and extra-land-drop plan into repeatable raw card draw, with excellent reliability here and a solid floor once any land drops continue.

Tatyova rewards the deck’s land-focused engine with accumulating cards every time lands enter, generating sustained advantage that strongly converts setup into momentum even if it is not the fastest closer alone.

Zendikar Resurgent.overall_comment: Zendikar Resurgent is a high-ceiling mana engine for this lands-heavy Tatyova deck that also turns creature casts into extra cards, though its seven-mana setup means it matters most once the deck is already established.

roles.description: It gives every land an additional mana for the rest of the game, providing powerful repeatable acceleration and excellent fixing in a lands-heavy deck, with its main constraint being a very late seven-mana entry point.

Its creature-cast trigger can snowball into multiple extra cards over time, and this deck plays enough creatures to benefit, though the draw starts only after a costly setup turn and depends on having creature spells to cast.
